message:
fix(wills-article): correct install-card gating description to match real JSM behavior

Step 4 of the wills-and-estate-planning article's setup section told readers
that opening the skill page without an active subscription would "bounce
[them] to the login screen." That does not match the real behavior of
`src/app/skills/[id]/page.tsx` in /dp/jeffreys-skills.md: unauthenticated
or unsubscribed viewers do not get redirected anywhere. They get the
detail URL they asked for, rendered as a full "Public Preview" variant
of the page (title, description, tags, skill visualization, preview
excerpt, Save button, What-You-Get sidebar) with the install/download
affordance replaced by a SubscriptionRequiredMessage block and a Sign-In
header link. No redirect ever happens for publicly listed skills, which
is exactly what the wills skill is — `isJeffreys: true` + `isPublic: true`
+ `accessTier: "public"`, which `canViewSkillDetailPreview()` in
`src/lib/skills/visibility.ts` maps to a preview-allowed state via
`isPubliclyListedSkill()`.

Rewrote the parenthetical to describe what the page actually does:

  page (the install card only appears for paying subscribers; if you are
  not yet signed in with an active subscription from step 3, the page
  shows a public preview of the skill instead).

Rationale for the new wording specifically:

  * Matches JSM's own non-technical guide copy verbatim in spirit
    (`src/app/guide/non-technical/page.tsx:284-286`: "Make sure you're
    signed in with the same account that holds your subscription — the
    install card only appears for paying subscribers.").
  * Leads with the positive ("the install card only appears for paying
    subscribers") rather than a failure state, since by the time a reader
    is on step 4 they have already completed step 3 and the parenthetical
    is only relevant if they clicked the link out of order.
  * Avoids UX/marketing jargon ("CTA", "paywall modal", "gate") — the
    article's audience is explicitly non-technical and the surrounding
    copy sticks to plain English throughout.

No other article surfaces repeat the old claim — the downloadable primer
`public/wills-and-estate-planning-primer.md` does not reference
login-screen behavior, and the Playwright specs covering the article
(`tests/e2e/wills-estate-article.spec.ts` plus `_diag-wills-*.spec.ts`)
do not pin the old string, so no companion edits or test updates are
required.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch: @@ -992,9 +992,10 @@ export function WillsEstateArticle() { > wills-and-estate-planning skill {" "} - page (the link only works once you are signed in with an - active subscription from step 3; otherwise it bounces to - the login screen). Click{" "} + page (the install card only appears for paying + subscribers; if you are not yet signed in with an active + subscription from step 3, the page shows a public preview + of the skill instead). Click{" "} Generate install prompt. Copy the block of text it gives you. Paste that block into your desktop app's chat box. Press enter. The agent reads it,
